reference.title: Data set categories and types
reference.shortdesc: You can allocate data sets with default characteristics assigned according to their categories and types.

refbody.section:
When you allocate a new data set, one option that is presented on the New Data Set wizard is Specify characteristics by usage type. With this option, you can select a category and type for your new data set and assigns data set characteristics appropriate to your choice. The following table lists the categories and types available and their default data set characteristics.Data set categories and types
Categories Types
SOURCE
ASM
Space units: BLOCKS
Primary Quantity: 300
Secondary Quantity: 100
Directory Blocks: 20
Record Format: FB
Record Length: 80
Block Size: 0
C/C++
Space units: BLOCKS
Primary Quantity: 300
Secondary Quantity: 100
Directory Blocks: 20
Record Format: FB
Record Length: 80
Block Size: 0
COBOL
Space units: BLOCKS
Primary Quantity: 300
Secondary Quantity: 100
Directory Blocks: 20
Record Format: FB
Record Length: 80
Block Size: 0
JCL
Space units: BLOCKS
Primary Quantity: 200
Secondary Quantity: 100
Directory Blocks: 20
Record Format: FB
Record Length: 80
Block Size: 0
PLI
Space units: BLOCKS
Primary Quantity: 300
Secondary Quantity: 100
Directory Blocks: 20
Record Format: FB
Record Length: 80
Block Size: 0
REXX
Space units: BLOCKS
Primary Quantity: 200
Secondary Quantity: 100
Directory Blocks: 20
Record Format: FB
Record Length: 80
Block Size: 0
LISTING
ASM
Space units: BLOCKS
Primary Quantity: 300
Secondary Quantity: 150
Directory Blocks: 20
Record Format: FBA
Record Length: 133
Block Size: 0

Record Length: 121
Block Size: 0
COBOL

Space units: BLOCKS

Block Size: 0
Others
ADATA [ASM]
Space units: BLOCKS
Primary Quantity: 300
Secondary Quantity: 100
Directory Blocks: 20
Record Format: VB
Record Length: 8188
Block Size: 0

Space units: BLOCKS
